results.possibleIdentity: Richard Liss was an engineer and technical professional active during the late 1960s and early 1970s. He was employed by Westinghouse Electric Corporation, specifically at their facility in Newark, New Jersey (likely the Relay-Instrument Division). His professional expertise focused on digital systems, logical design, and electronic design. Liss is noted for his association with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization founded to foster collaborations between the scientific/engineering communities and artists. Within the E.A.T. framework, Liss was part of a network of technical experts who provided specialized knowledge—such as logical and digital system design—to assist artists in realizing complex technological works. His professional profile was maintained in the E.A.T. Technical Services files, which used index cards to match engineers' skills and locations with artists' project requirements.
